audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim love-obedience, land-rain, second-Shema paragraph, and blessing-curse discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for eyewitness generation, Egypt and wilderness memory, Dathan/Abiram judgment, land dependent on rain, Divine care, second paragraph of Shema, ecological consequence, teaching/signs, territorial promise, and Gerizim/Ebal blessing-curse ceremony.

verses.restored: You shall love YHWH your Divine and keep his charge, statutes, judgments, and commands all days.
Know today—for not with your children who did not know and did not see discipline/instruction of YHWH your Divine, his greatness, strong hand, and outstretched arm—
and his signs and works he did in midst of Egypt to Pharaoh king of Egypt and all his land,
and what he did to army of Egypt, its horses and chariots, how he caused waters of Sea of Reeds to flow over their faces when they pursued after you, and YHWH destroyed them until this day,
and what he did for you in wilderness until you came to this place,
and what he did to Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab son of Reuben, how earth opened its mouth and swallowed them, their households, tents, and every living thing at their feet, in midst of all Israel.
For your eyes are the ones seeing all great work of YHWH that he did.
You shall keep all command I command you today, so you may be strong and enter and possess land where you are crossing to possess it,
and so you may lengthen days upon ground YHWH swore to your fathers to give to them and their seed, land flowing with milk and honey.
For land where you are entering to possess it is not like land of Egypt from which you came, where you sowed your seed and watered it by foot like garden of vegetables.
But land where you are crossing to possess is land of mountains and valleys; from rain of heavens it drinks water,
a land YHWH your Divine seeks/carefully attends; eyes of YHWH your Divine are continually upon it, from beginning of year to end of year.
It shall be, if you diligently listen to my commands that I command you today, to love YHWH your Divine and serve him with all your heart and all your soul/life,
then I will give rain of your land in its season, early rain and latter rain, and you shall gather your grain, new wine, and oil.
I will give grass in your field for your livestock, and you shall eat and be satisfied.
Guard yourselves lest your heart be deceived and you turn aside and serve other gods and bow to them,
and anger of YHWH burn against you and he shut heavens so there is no rain and ground does not give produce, and you perish quickly from good land YHWH gives you.
You shall set these words of mine upon your heart and upon your soul/life, bind them as sign upon your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.
You shall teach them to your children, speaking of them when you sit in your house, when you walk on way, when you lie down, and when you rise.
You shall write them upon doorposts of your house and upon your gates,
so your days and days of your children may multiply upon ground YHWH swore to your fathers to give them, as days of heavens upon earth.
For if you diligently keep all this command I command you to do, to love YHWH your Divine, to walk in all his ways, and to cling to him,
then YHWH will dispossess all these nations from before you, and you shall possess nations greater and mightier than you.
Every place sole of your foot treads shall be yours, from wilderness and Lebanon, from river, river Euphrates, as far as western sea shall be your border.
No man shall stand before you; YHWH your Divine will put dread of you and fear of you upon face of all land where you tread, as he spoke to you.
See, I set before you today blessing and curse:
blessing, if you listen to commands of YHWH your Divine that I command you today;
and curse, if you do not listen to commands of YHWH your Divine and turn aside from way I command you today, to go after other gods you have not known.
It shall be, when YHWH your Divine brings you into land where you are entering to possess it, you shall put blessing upon Mount Gerizim and curse upon Mount Ebal.
Are they not across Jordan, beyond way of sunset, in land of Canaanite dwelling in Arabah opposite Gilgal, beside oaks of Moreh?
For you are crossing Jordan to enter and possess land YHWH your Divine gives you; you shall possess it and dwell in it.
You shall keep to do all statutes and judgments I set before you today.

translation_notes: The chapter addresses an eyewitness generation and uses communal memory to ground responsibility; it must not turn descendants into targets of inherited blame.
Dathan and Abiram's household destruction is recalled without resolving collective-punishment concerns raised in Numbers 16 and 26.
The land's dependence on rain creates ecological vulnerability and covenant attention, but drought must not be simplistically blamed on individual sin or used against climate-disaster victims.
Verses 13-21 form the second paragraph of the Shema in Jewish liturgy and must be honored within living Jewish practice without supersession or appropriation.
Love, service, teaching, tefillin, and mezuzah practices remain embodied covenant memory.
The expansive territorial promise and imposed dread are ancient conquest rhetoric and cannot function as modern borders or authorization for displacement.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 11 to blame drought, famine, climate disaster, or household tragedy on victims; to appropriate Jewish prayer; or to justify territorial expansion and fear-based rule. Center love, teaching, ecological humility, daily remembrance, and the ethical choice set before the community.
